title: Review: jmap/space_set correctness, contact_set, ecb97a8–18000ea
notes:
Maintainer risk: LOW — one P2 survives to HEAD (bare invalidArguments with no field name). The known gaps in intermediate commits (member-only existence check, description:null no-op) were all fixed in subsequent commits in the same push. Chain is coherent.
Confidence: 80% chance of no missed bug. Uncertainty: (1) contact_query_changes still queries all contacts to compute total even when returning empty added[] — correct behavior but unnecessary work at scale; (2) ChatContact/changes uses 'removed' not 'destroyed' — consistent within this codebase but diverges from standard RFC 8620 Foo/changes key name. Neither is a bug given the custom capability namespace. Would raise confidence if: there were a test for the saturating_add case with i64::MAX inputs (test_contact_query_large_position_limit_does_not_panic covers it — good) and a test for contact_changes with sinceState slightly stale but not 0.
